prompt: a tug of war betweeen ancient kabuki actors (yakusha-e), one group with very less members the other with more, the group with less members is close to middle line and the other is distant from it, clearly vi one group with red attire and the other with blue attire,  bold lines and flat, in Japanese Edo-period woodblock prints / ukiyo-e (1603–1868) style., totally  side view from a distant view and more of a 2D drawing less of 3D .Negative prompt: no text, lines or diagrams or manuscripts
tradition_approach: I targeted bold contour lines, flat color blocks, minimal shading, and simplified forms—hallmarks of ukiyo-e—because they preserve visual clarity, making the class-imbalnce metaphor legible while remaining faithful to the tradition’s compositional grammar
